item.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
item.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: Former BA boss Walsh returns as chief of Indigo
item.description: Company’s previous head abruptly quit last year after poor planning triggered thousands of flight cancellations

item.title: These Two Countries With Social Media Bans Aren’t Happy With Google and Meta
item.description: Indonesia says the two companies are violating its law that bars children under 16 from the social media. Australia has similar concerns.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
A Cat-and-Mouse Game of Russian Internet Restrictions and Evasion
item.description: As the Kremlin spends heavily on censorship technology, Russians are scrambling to find new ways to circumvent the limits.

item.title: U.S. Senators Press Taiwan to Raise Military Spending, as China Protests
item.description: Four visiting senators urged Taiwan to break an impasse over a $40 billion budget proposal, highlighting concerns in Washington about the threat from China.

item.title: Japan deploys first long-range missiles as it steps up deterrence
item.description: Domestically built munitions with 1,000km range could reach Chinese coast

item.title: Huge fires at Russian oil facilities following Ukraine strikes, satellite images show
item.description: BBC Verify examines recent damage to three key Russian oil export facilities near the Baltic Sea.

item.title: How to crack it: 13 amazing things to do in April
item.description: From bakeries to bling, HTSI writers select the best new launches

item.title: US presses UK and allies to secure quantum computing supply chains
item.description: US CTO Ethan Klein seeks British co-operation amid stand-off over wider tech pact

item.title: Kuwaiti Tanker Full of Oil Struck Off Dubai Day After Trump’s Threats
item.description: The vessel caught fire and sustained damage, its owner said on Tuesday, adding that there was the potential for an oil spill in surrounding waters. On Monday, President Trump warned Iran that without a deal he would order attacks on infrastructure targets.

item.title: Eurovision Song Contest launches first-ever Asia edition
item.description: Broadcasters from 10 countries, including South Korea and the Philippines, will be taking part.

item.title: Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Set Ablaze After Attack Off the Coast of Dubai
item.description: The attack damaged the hull, the tanker's owner said. No oil spill was detected, according to the authorities in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Eurozone inflation jumps to 2.5% in March
item.description: Middle East energy shock pushes annual rate past European Central Bank’s 2% target

item.title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
item.description: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

item.title: FirstFT: Pete Hegseth’s broker looked to buy defence fund before Iran attack
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US petrol prices rise above $4 a gallon while Unilever and McCormick in talks to create $60bn food group

item.title: Zelensky says allies asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy
item.description: Ukraine's president says partners asked if the attacks could be "reduced" as prices soar due to the Iran war.

item.title: Whoop hits $10bn valuation as health tracking takes off
item.description: Wearable device maker eyes IPO after raising $575mn from sovereign wealth funds and top athletes

item.title: 'Felt close to death': Indian seafarers detained in Iran return home
item.description: The crew members, whose ship was seized in December, got their release order a day before the war began.

item.title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
item.description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

item.title: Inside Russia During an Internet Crackdown
item.description: Our international correspondent Valerie Hopkins walks us through how she connects to the internet in Russia as the Kremlin clamps down and Moscow experiences major internet blackouts.

item.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
item.description: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

item.title: First Canadian Astronaut Will Travel to the Moon Amid Fraying U.S.-Canada Relations
item.description: Canada will send its first astronaut to the moon on a joint mission with the United States, but back on Earth, the relationship between the two countries is fraying.

item.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
item.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

item.title: Lib Dems borrow from Macron playbook for May 7
item.description: Though the party is fifth nationally, Ed Davey thinks sticking to the centre ground can deliver local election gains

item.title: Hundreds of my abusers are still out there, says victim as grooming inquiry launched
item.description: There needs to be accountability for the failings that led to this mass exploitation of children in UK, says survivor.

item.title: UK growers warn of cucumber and tomato shortages as gas prices surge
item.description: Producers warn of empty shelves if supermarkets do not raise prices to help offset costs linked to Iran war

item.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
item.description: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.

item.title: When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A.I. Can Help Answer That
item.description: Experts use artificial intelligence to analyze data, plus thousands of crowdsourced photos, to forecast the prized flowers, which are a multibillion-dollar attraction.

item.title: Starmer gives doctors 48 hours to cancel strike or lose new jobs package
item.description: The prime minister says the NHS could lose 1,000 extra training places if resident doctors go ahead with a six-day strike next week.

item.title: Ghana sack manager Addo, 72 days before World Cup
item.description: Ghana dismiss manager Otto Addo, 72 days before the start of the 2026 World Cup, following a run of defeats.

item.title: Inflation worries become growth worries
item.description: A longer war makes long bonds interesting

item.title: Aviation regulator rejects Heathrow’s £10bn spending plan
item.description: Civil Aviation Authority says average charges per passenger should stay broadly flat over next five years

item.title: Asia turns to coal as Iran war chokes off gas supplies
item.description: Countries shrug off environmental concerns to fire up use of polluting fuel as Gulf supplies dwindle

item.title: Kosovo in euphoria as they dream of World Cup history
item.description: Having gained independence in 2008, Kosovo are one game away from qualifying for the World Cup and sparking "the biggest celebrations ever seen in Pristina".

item.title: EU ministers commemorate Bucha massacre as Ukraine peace talks stall
item.description: Also in this newsletter: the former Putin translator overseeing the Hungarian election observation mission

item.title: Private capital’s great disappointment era begins
item.description: The $22tn industry is facing the prospect that returns will fall far short of the expectations of investors

item.title: Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war
item.description: International official holdings at New York Federal Reserve fall to lowest level since 2012

item.title: BlackRock looks at HSBC’s Canary Wharf tower for new London HQ
item.description: Asset manager is seeking at least 600,000 sq ft as demand for high-end offices rises

item.title: Cracks appear in US-UK security co-operation after Trump-Starmer tensions
item.description: Differences over war in Iran are hitting working relationships between diplomats, officials and military personnel

item.title: Swiss lawmakers signal compromise on $22bn UBS capital plan
item.description: Decision could be published in April with foreign capital requirements component moving to parliament for debate

item.title: ‘Pump anxiety’ from soaring fuel prices prompts surge in EV interest
item.description: Increased costs since outbreak of Middle East conflict prompt more test drives and advert views

item.title: BlackRock to boost investment in NHS property
item.description: Asset manager teams up with Greater Manchester Pension Fund for £1bn joint venture as government seeks more private finance

item.title: Government to overhaul non-crime hate incident rules
item.description: The Home Office says updated guidance will end the policing of "everyday arguments" online.

item.title: 'A succulent Chinese meal' - iconic Australian quote immortalised in national film archive
item.description: The theatrical monologue given by Jack Karlson as he was arrested in 1991 has been officially recognised as a "lasting piece of cultural folklore".

item.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
item.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

item.title: Social media firms must better enforce Australia under-16 ban, watchdog says
item.description: Regulator eSafety says it has concerns about how Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are complying with the ban.

item.title: Israel Passes Law to Hang Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
item.description: Experts say the law was written in such a way as to ensure that it was unlikely to ever apply to Jewish extremists who commit similar crimes.

item.title: Mexico demands answers after another migrant dies in ICE custody
item.description: President Sheinbaum vows to take action after the death of a Mexican last week at a California facility.

item.title: Why Did the U.S. Allow a Russian Oil Tanker Through Its Cuba Blockade?
item.description: The ship’s arrival would bring Cuba some badly needed fuel. It also marks a shift from U.S. efforts to block oil shipments and pressure the Cuban government.

item.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
item.description: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.

item.title: Binyamin Netanyahu is down—but not out
item.description: Ahead of an election due in October, a divided opposition and the war could hand him a lifeline

item.title: Gazan mother reunited with evacuated daughter after two years
item.description: The girl is among at least eight children who were premature newborns when they were evacuated during heavy fighting in Gaza at the start of the war.

item.title: Most Syrian refugees in Germany expected to return home in three years, Merz says
item.description: Merz’s government has taken a tougher line on refugees and migrants, amid surging support for the anti-immigration AfD party.

item.title: Céline Dion announces comeback shows: 'I'm so ready for this'
item.description: The star was diagnosed with a rare condition that affects her singing voice and her ability to walk.

item.title: Two Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed in explosion in Lebanon
item.description: It is the second fatal incident in the last 24 hours, Unifil says, after Israel expanded its operation against Hezbollah.

item.title: Luis Carlos Rúa Fought Colombia’s Corruption in an Elephant Costume. Now He’s a Senator.
item.description: After years of anonymously documenting abandoned public works projects in Colombia, Luis Carlos Rúa revealed himself days before his election.

item.title: 48-hour curfew imposed after attack on bar in Nigerian city
item.description: The raid led to revenge attacks in Jos, with 22 reported dead so far in one of the most volatile parts of the country.

item.title: For China’s officials, the goal was once growth. Now it’s loyalty
item.description: An obsession with GDP is replaced by an obsession with Xi Jinping

item.title: Remove VAT from energy bills for three years, Tories urge
item.description: There has been a renewed focus on energy costs since the outbreak of the war in Iran.

item.title: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
item.description: Rabiu Kwankwaso joins the ADC, a coalition poised to take on President Tinubu in the 2027 elections.

item.title: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
item.description: Locals say the structure, in a school complex, was in use despite lying unfinished for a number of years.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Americans of Political Killings for Hire Overseas
item.description: Three men working for an American security contractor say the United Arab Emirates paid them to carry out targeted killings in Yemen. One of their targets is suing them in U.S. federal court.

item.title: Spirit of Caerphilly by-election could propel Plaid into government, says leader
item.description: Plaid Cymru leader launches Senedd election campaign, comparing it to fight to win Caerphilly in October.

item.title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
item.description: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Oil rises and Asia shares slide as Iran war enters fifth week
item.description: It comes after Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen joined the conflict by striking Israel over the weekend.

item.title: Spain closes airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran war
item.description: It follows a decision by the Spanish government to deny the US use of the two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.

item.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
item.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

item.title: Russian oil tanker reaches Cuba after Trump appears to loosen blockade
item.description: The tanker has reached Cuban waters just hours after Donald Trump said he had "no problem" with it.

item.title: Defection MP locked out of office loses court case
item.description: Andrew Rosindell was locked out of the Conservatives' Romford constituency office in January.

item.title: He Led Congo for 18 Years. Now, Joseph Kabila Is a Hunted Man.
item.description: Joseph Kabila, the former president, faces the death penalty after the government convicted him of treason last year. He says the charges are bogus.

item.title: Welsh Labour promises NHS will see people within 48 hours in manifesto
item.description: Leader Eluned Morgan made her pledge as she launched her party's Senedd election manifesto in Swansea.

item.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
item.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

item.title: The Venezuela Donald Trump “runs” is a land of surreal contrasts
item.description: Animal spirits are stirring. The opposition has hope. The regime’s softening of repression is uneven

item.title: Korir banned for five years over doping admission
item.description: Former New York marathon winner Albert Korir of Kenya is banned for five years after admitting to doping.

item.title: Government must do more to help detained Brits in UAE, advocacy group says
item.description: The UK Foreign Office says it is giving consular assistance to "a small number of British nationals" that have been detained there after allegedly taking pictures of Iranian attacks.

item.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Israel Pulls Battalion From West Bank After Soldier Talks of ‘Revenge’
item.description: While detaining a CNN crew, a soldier said that Israeli troops were motivated by “revenge” against Palestinians and that all of the West Bank was “for the Jews.”

item.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
item.description: The disruption in oil supplies has caused shortages and concerns in several African countries.

item.title: PM launches Labour's local election campaign with focus on cost of living
item.description: Keir Starmer says the 7 May elections take place against an "uncertain" backdrop with wars in Ukraine and Iran.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

item.title: Russia expels British diplomat over spying allegations
item.description: A UK Foreign Office spokesman accuses Russia of an "aggressive and co-ordinated campaign of harassment against British diplomats".

item.title: How office chair racing in Japan is attracting thrill-seekers
item.description: No souped-up chairs are allowed in this competition.

item.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
item.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

item.title: Can the ‘Dubai Dream’ Survive the War? Residents Say Life Goes On.
item.description: The Emirates’ largest city pitched itself to foreign workers and tourists as a sun-soaked safe haven in a volatile region. War has challenged that image.

item.title: Fugitive Dezi Freeman shot dead by Australian police after seven months in hiding
item.description: Freeman has been wanted since the fatal shooting of two police officers in Porepunkah in August.

item.title: Fuel tax halved and free public transport offered as war pushes up prices in Australia
item.description: Victoria and Tasmania are incentivising commuters not to drive in a bid to ease cost pressures.

item.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing nominated as president
item.description: The military leader, who has been sanctioned by Western countries, is now certain to get the job.

item.title: Why Chinese tech companies are racing to set up in Hong Kong
item.description: Mainland firms are using the territory to test products and as a springboard for global expansion.

item.title: Thieves steal Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse paintings in three-minute Italian heist
item.description: The paintings were taken from a museum in Parma by four masked men in the middle of the night, police say.

item.title: Educated and employed but still struggling: India's middle class under strain
item.description: Once aspirational, India's middle class today are finding fewer avenues for their ambition.

item.title: Italy investigates Sephora and Benefit over marketing skincare to children
item.description: The Italian Competition Authority said luxury goods giant LVMH appears to have adopted "particularly insidious" marketing strategies.

item.title: Councils pressed to use universal parking app to cut 'unfair' fines
item.description: The National Parking Platform already has 15 councils on board with more in talks to join.

item.title: All sides in the Gulf war are at risk of overplaying their hands
item.description: The costs of dragging out the conflict could be very high indeed

item.title: Is Bollywood’s latest megahit propaganda for Narendra Modi?
item.description: “Dhurandhar: The Revenge” portrays India extracting a price for Pakistani perfidy

item.title: Three arrested in Paris after attempted bomb attack outside Bank of America
item.description: France's anti-terrorism prosecutor's office said it had immediately taken over the investigation.

item.title: Caf general secretary steps down amid Afcon final fallout
item.description: Veron Mosengo-Omba, a controversial figure, leaves at a turbulent time for African football.

item.title: 'I was naive,' says minister who quit over Labour Together claims
item.description: Josh Simons resigned after facing claims a think tank he used to run commissioned a report into journalists' backgrounds.

item.title: China’s leadership is about to be shaken up
item.description: The changes will affect every level. Except one

item.title: Cut taxes on energy bills before giving bailouts, Badenoch says
item.description: The Tory leader refuses to rule out direct payments to households if bills spike but says this would come at a cost.

item.title: Schools do not have enough staff to make SEND reforms work, union warns
item.description: The National Education Union says schools need more funding to be able to make all classrooms inclusive.

item.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
item.description: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.

item.title: How deepfake porn scandal surrounding TV star rocked Germany
item.description: Collien Fernandes has accused her ex-husband of spreading images of her online, but he has categorically denied it.

item.title: India restoring a centuries-old royal kitchen that never stopped serving food
item.description: The royal kitchen still serves food, but the complex has been worn down by time.

item.title: The Houthis’ attack on Israel heralds escalation of the Iran war
item.description: Yemen’s Shia militia could threaten to choke the Red Sea

item.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships found days after disappearing
item.description: The crew are safe and will complete their mission, a spokesman for the organisers said.

item.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
item.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

item.title: Tears and defiance as Lebanese family bury child, 11, killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Jawad Younes and his uncle Ragheb were laid to rest on Saturday after their family compound was hit a day earlier.

item.title: Senegal parade Afcon trophy despite title controversy
item.description: Senegal parade the Africa Cup of Nations trophy before Saturday's friendly match against Peru in Paris - despite being stripped of the title earlier this month.

item.title: Twenty-two migrants die off Greek coast after six days at sea
item.description: Poor weather and a lack of food and water contributed to the deaths, the Greek coastguard says.
